summary: CUNY's six-year graduation rate for associate degree students peaked at 39.8% in Fiscal 2023 before declining two years straight to 36.5% in Fiscal 2025. The decline follows a broader enrollment slide — CUNY community college enrollment sat at just 66% of pre-pandemic levels, per the City Comptroller's analysis — and comes as the City enacted $155 million in cuts across Fiscal 2023 alone (235 lost positions), plus a further $41 million in permanent annual cuts proposed for Fiscal 2024–2026, threatening completion programs like ASAP that have historically driven CUNY's graduation-rate gains.

sources.label: Center for an Urban Future, "Closing NYC's College Attainment Gap"

oneLiner: decreasing since FY23, alongside a steep enrollment slide and City budget cuts to CUNY
